migrate_legacy.title: Migrate from Marstek Venus
migrate_legacy.description: Found {count} existing Marstek Venus configuration(s). Migrating to Omnibattery keeps all your entities, history and settings intact. Continue?

user.title: Configure Omnibattery
user.description: Select the sensor that measures grid import/export power. Optionally, configure the solar forecast sensor for advanced features.

data_description.consumption_sensor: Sensor that indicates imported (+) or exported (-) power to the grid. The controller will adjust batteries to keep this value near 0W.
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). If configured here, it will be used automatically for predictive charging and solar charge delay.
data_description.solar_production_sensor: Real-time PV production power sensor (W/kW) from an external inverter not wired through the battery MPPT inputs. Used to show the Solar node in the dashboard energy-flow diagram. Leave empty if your panels feed the battery MPPT directly.
data_description.meter_inverted: Enable this option if your meter uses the opposite convention: positive = export, negative = import. The unit (W/kW) is detected automatically.
data_description.max_contracted_power: Total contracted power in watts. System won't exceed this limit when charging to avoid tripping the breaker

batteries.title: Number of batteries
batteries.description: How many batteries do you have installed?

battery_brand.title: Battery {battery_num} — Brand
battery_brand.description: Select the brand and type of battery {battery_num}.

data_description.brand: Marstek Venus: Modbus TCP. Zendure SolarFlow: local HTTP (enable HEMS in the Zendure app first).

battery_connection.title: Configure battery {battery_num} — Connection (Marstek)
battery_connection.description: Enter connection details and battery model for battery {battery_num}.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.host: IP address of the battery on your local network. Leave empty if you use a serial port.
data_description.slave_id: Modbus slave/unit id (default 1). Change only when a Modbus proxy maps several batteries to one IP:port using distinct slave ids.
data_description.battery_version: Select your battery model version. Ev2 for older models, Ev3 for newer models with updated register map, A (Venus A) or D (Venus D) for hybrid inverter models
data_description.serial_port: USB-RS485 adapter path, e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 or COM3. Fill this instead of the IP address to use Modbus RTU over serial; leave empty for TCP.
data_description.queued_gateway_compatibility: For Venus v2 over Modbus TCP only. Restores MVEM's retry path: one send per transaction ID and up to three attempts with a fresh ID after a failed, timed-out or incomplete response. Leave disabled for direct connections, Elfin EW11, serial RTU and normal gateways.

battery_connection_zendure.title: Configure battery {battery_num} — Connection (Zendure)
battery_connection_zendure.description: Enter connection details for the Zendure SolarFlow battery {battery_num}. Make sure HEMS is enabled in the Zendure app.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.host: IP address of the Zendure device on your local network

battery_connection_esphome.title: Configure battery {battery_num} — Connection (LilyGo/ESPHome)
battery_connection_esphome.description: Select the ESPHome device (LilyGo RS485 bridge) that exposes Marstek battery {battery_num}. It must run the marstek-lilygo-rs485 firmware with the stock entity names.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.esphome_device: The LilyGo T-CAN485 device flashed with the Marstek ESPHome firmware

battery_limits.title: Configure battery {battery_num} — Limits
battery_limits.description: Enter power and SOC limits for battery {battery_num}.

data_description.max_charge_power: Maximum power allowed to charge the battery
data_description.max_discharge_power: Maximum power allowed to discharge the battery
data_description.max_soc: Maximum state of charge - battery won't charge above this value
data_description.min_soc: Minimum state of charge - battery won't discharge below this value
data_description.charge_hysteresis_percent: Always on (minimum 2%). After reaching the top, the battery won't charge again until SOC drops to (Max SOC − hysteresis). Ex: Max SOC 100% with 2% = no charge until 98%.
data_description.backup_offgrid_threshold: Offgrid load above this value is treated as an active backup event, excluding the battery from PD control. Set higher if you have permanent loads (router, cameras) on the offgrid port.
data_description.full_charge_voltage_taper_enabled: When the target is 100%, limit charge to 95 W from 3.48 V max cell voltage and stop at 3.58 V to measure imbalance after 60 seconds.
data_description.battery_capacity_kwh: Nominal usable capacity of the battery pack. Used to calculate stored energy and efficiency from SOC. Leave at 0 if unknown.

time_slots.title: Discharge time slots
time_slots.description: Do you want to configure time slots? If configured, batteries will ONLY discharge during these slots. Without slots, they operate at all times.

add_time_slot.title: Time slot {slot_num}
add_time_slot.description: Define when and how the batteries are allowed to operate. The ticks control each direction, SOC and power. Manual mode forces an exact power, bypassing the PD algorithm.

data_description.start_time: Slot start time (24h). Midnight crossing supported.
data_description.battery_scope: Select 'all' to apply to every battery, or pick a specific one.
data_description.allow_charge: If checked, charge is allowed during the slot. If NO slot allows charge, charge is unrestricted by default.
data_description.allow_discharge: If checked, discharge is allowed during the slot. If NO slot allows discharge, discharge is unrestricted by default.
data_description.soc_override_enabled: If checked, the slot overrides the battery's max/min SOC inside the window.
data_description.power_override_enabled: If checked, the slot limits or forces the max power.
data_description.mode: PD: the predictive algorithm caps power using the slot value. Manual: forced discharge or charge at the configured power.

add_time_slot_details.title: Slot {slot_num} details
add_time_slot_details.description: Configure SOC and/or power limits per battery. Every battery has its own sliders. Mapping: {battery_map}. SOC min range: 12–30%. SOC max range: 80–100%. Power range: 100 W up to each battery’s hardware max.

add_more_slots.title: Add more time slots
add_more_slots.description: You currently have {current_slots} configured slot(s). You can add up to {max_slots} slots in total.

excluded_devices.title: Special Device Management
excluded_devices.description: Configure devices with special management: you can EXCLUDE devices that should NOT be powered by battery, or ADD devices that SHOULD be powered by battery even if they're not in the home consumption sensor.

add_excluded_device.title: Special Device {device_num}
add_excluded_device.description: Configure how the battery should manage this device.

data.included_in_consumption: Consumption is included in home consumption sensor
data.allow_solar_surplus: Allow to use solar surplus (do not charge battery)

data_description.power_sensor: Power sensor (W) for the device. For EV chargers without power telemetry, select the state sensor that reads 'Charging' when the vehicle is charging.
data_description.included_in_consumption: ✓ CHECKED = Home sensor ALREADY includes this device → Battery will NOT power it (excluded). ✗ UNCHECKED = Home sensor doesn't see it → Battery WILL power it (additional)
data_description.allow_solar_surplus: If checked, the device will be able to consume energy directly from solar panels (surplus) without the battery trying to compensate. Recommended for high consumption devices like EV chargers.
data_description.ev_charger_no_telemetry: ✓ CHECKED = The selected sensor reports the charging state ('Charging'/'Cargando'). When EV charging is detected, the battery pauses for 5 minutes so the vehicle can draw full power; afterwards the battery may only charge from solar surplus and will never discharge while the EV is charging.

add_more_excluded_devices.title: Add more excluded devices
add_more_excluded_devices.description: You currently have {current_devices} excluded device(s). You can add up to {max_devices} devices total.

predictive_charging.title: Predictive Grid Charging
predictive_charging.description: Do you want to enable intelligent grid charging? This feature allows charging batteries from the grid during off-peak hours when today's solar forecast is insufficient.

predictive_charging_mode.title: Predictive Charging Mode
predictive_charging_mode.description: Select how the system decides when to charge from the grid.

data_description.predictive_charging_mode: Time Slot: charge during a fixed nightly window. Dynamic Pricing: automatically select the cheapest hours from a price sensor. Real-Time Price: activate charging instantly whenever the current price is below a threshold — no scheduling needed.

predictive_charging_config.title: Predictive Charging — Time Slot
predictive_charging_config.description: Configure when and how to charge batteries from the grid based on solar forecast. You can set up to 3 charging windows — fill only window 1 to keep a single window, or add windows 2 and 3 as needed.

data_description.start_time: Start time of charging window (e.g., 01:00, off-peak tariff)
data_description.days: Days when grid charging is allowed
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing the solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). If you have solar panels, configure this sensor — the system will only charge from the grid when solar forecast is insufficient. If you have no solar panels, leave this empty — the system will charge whenever battery energy is not enough for the day. Not required if you already configured this sensor in the initial setup step — it will be used automatically.
data_description.predictive_safety_margin_kwh: Extra energy buffer added to the consumption forecast before deciding whether to charge. Useful when your solar forecast tends to be optimistic. Set to 0 to disable (default). Capped at total battery capacity.
data_description.predictive_grid_charge_margin_pct: Extra % charged from the grid on top of the solar-deficit, to hedge against optimistic solar forecasts. E.g. a 2 kWh grid need at 50% charges 3 kWh. 0 to disable (default). Capped at the gap to max SOC.

dynamic_pricing_config.title: Predictive Charging — Dynamic Pricing
dynamic_pricing_config.description: Configure the electricity price sensor and parameters for automatic cheap-hour selection.

data.dp_price_discharge_control: Only discharge when price is above threshold

data_description.price_integration_type: Select your electricity price provider integration. Tibber is service-based: it reads prices from the tibber.get_prices service, so the price sensor below can be left empty.
data_description.price_sensor: Sensor providing electricity prices (e.g., sensor.nordpool_kwh_es_eur_3_10_0 for Nordpool, sensor.esios_pvpc for PVPC, or your CKW sensor for Switzerland)
data_description.max_price_threshold: Slots above this price will not be selected. When price-gated discharge is enabled, this value is also used as the discharge threshold. Use the same unit as the sensor: €/kWh for Nordpool/PVPC, CHF/kWh for CKW. Leave empty to use all slots and use the daily average as the discharge threshold.
data_description.dp_price_discharge_control: When enabled, the battery only discharges when the current electricity price exceeds the configured maximum price threshold. If no threshold is configured, it uses today's average price computed automatically from the price sensor. If discharge time slots are configured, both conditions must be met.
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing the solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). If you have solar panels, configure this sensor — the system will only charge from the grid when solar forecast is insufficient. If you have no solar panels, leave this empty — the system will charge whenever battery energy is not enough for the day. Not required if you already configured this sensor in the initial setup step — it will be used automatically.
data_description.predictive_safety_margin_kwh: Extra energy buffer added to the consumption forecast before deciding whether to charge. Useful when your solar forecast tends to be optimistic. Set to 0 to disable (default). Capped at total battery capacity.
data_description.predictive_grid_charge_margin_pct: Extra % charged from the grid on top of the solar-deficit, to hedge against optimistic solar forecasts. E.g. a 2 kWh grid need at 50% charges 3 kWh. 0 to disable (default). Capped at the gap to max SOC.

realtime_price_config.title: Predictive Charging — Real-Time Price
realtime_price_config.description: Configure the price sensor and threshold. The system reads the current price every few seconds and starts or stops grid charging immediately — no daily schedule needed.

data.rt_price_discharge_control: Only discharge when price is above threshold

data_description.price_sensor: Sensor whose state is the current electricity price (e.g. sensor.esios_pvpc, sensor.nordpool_kwh_es_eur_3_10_0). Any sensor that exposes the current period price as its state works.
data_description.max_price_threshold: Charging activates when the current price is at or below this value. Use the same unit as your price sensor (e.g. €/kWh for Nordpool/PVPC, CHF/kWh for CKW). Leave empty if using the daily average sensor below.
data_description.average_price_sensor: If provided, this sensor's state is used as the threshold instead of the fixed value above — useful when your price integration already exposes a daily average. Leave empty to use the fixed threshold.
data_description.rt_price_discharge_control: When enabled, the battery only discharges when the current electricity price exceeds the threshold (fixed or daily average). If discharge time slots are configured, both conditions must be met.
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing the solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). If you have solar panels, configure this sensor — the system will only charge from the grid when solar forecast is insufficient. Not required if you already configured this sensor in the initial setup step — it will be used automatically.
data_description.predictive_safety_margin_kwh: Extra energy buffer added to the consumption forecast before deciding whether to charge. Useful when your solar forecast tends to be optimistic. Set to 0 to disable (default). Capped at total battery capacity.
data_description.predictive_grid_charge_margin_pct: Extra % charged from the grid on top of the solar-deficit, to hedge against optimistic solar forecasts. E.g. a 2 kWh grid need at 50% charges 3 kWh. 0 to disable (default). Capped at the gap to max SOC.

weekly_full_charge.title: Weekly Full Charge
weekly_full_charge.description: Do you want to enable weekly full battery charge for cell balancing? This feature charges batteries to 100% once per week to balance the cells.

weekly_full_charge_config.title: Weekly Full Charge Configuration
weekly_full_charge_config.description: Select the day of the week when batteries should charge to 100% for cell balancing. After reaching 100%, the system reverts to your configured maximum charge limit.

data_description.weekly_full_charge_day: Day when batteries will charge to 100% regardless of configured max SOC. This helps balance battery cells.

charge_delay.title: Solar charge delay
charge_delay.description: Enable solar charge delay? This delays battery charging until the solar energy balance indicates it's needed, exporting excess solar to grid in the meantime.

charge_delay_config.title: Charge delay configuration
charge_delay_config.description: Configure the safety margin and solar forecast sensor for the charge delay.

data_description.delay_safety_margin_h: Hours before sunset by which charging must be complete. Higher values unlock charging earlier.
data_description.delay_soc_setpoint_enabled: When enabled, the battery charges freely up to the configured SOC; above it the solar delay activates.
data_description.delay_soc_setpoint: The SOC the battery must reach before the solar delay kicks in. Minimum is 12 % — the Venus battery minimum discharge SOC.
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). Not required if you already configured this sensor in the initial setup step — it will be used automatically.

temp_charge_limit.title: Temperature charge limit
temp_charge_limit.description: Reduce charge power when a battery gets hot? Above a temperature limit charging is throttled proportionally and restored as the battery cools.

temp_charge_limit_config.title: Temperature charge limit configuration
temp_charge_limit_config.description: Set the temperature at which charging starts to throttle, the band over which it ramps down, and the minimum charge power (as a percentage of normal).

data_description.temp_charge_limit_c: Charging runs at full power at or below this temperature; above it the derate begins.
data_description.temp_charge_limit_band_c: Temperature range above the limit over which charge power ramps down to the minimum.
data_description.temp_charge_limit_floor_pct: Charge power at the limit plus the band, as a percentage of the normal charge ceiling. 0% stops charging entirely when very hot.
data_description.temp_limit_apply_discharge: Apply the same temperature derate to discharge power. Discharge tolerates heat better, so this shares the charge threshold as a compromise; mainly it keeps discharge under the BMS hard cutoff.

capacity_protection.title: Peak Shaving Mode
capacity_protection.description: When enabled, if battery SOC drops below a threshold, the system conserves energy by only discharging to offset consumption above a peak limit.

capacity_protection_config.title: Peak Shaving Configuration
capacity_protection_config.description: Configure the SOC threshold below which peak shaving activates, and the peak power limit. The battery will only discharge to keep grid import at or below the peak limit.

data_description.capacity_protection_soc_threshold: When average battery SOC drops below this value, peak shaving activates. The battery will stop discharging for normal consumption and only cover peaks above the limit.
data_description.capacity_protection_limit: Grid import power threshold. When house consumption exceeds this value and peak shaving is active, the battery discharges only the excess above this limit.

hourly_balance.title: Hourly Net Balance
hourly_balance.description:
Enable hourly net balance control? This feature tracks grid import/export per hour and automatically adjusts the battery setpoint to achieve a target net energy balance.

⚠️ **This feature is only useful in Spain**, under the hourly surplus-compensation scheme (Royal Decree 244/2019), where grid surplus is settled hour by hour. In markets with feed-in tariffs or annual net metering it provides no benefit and may cause lost export revenue and unnecessary battery cycling.

Detected Home Assistant country: **{country}**.

pd_advanced.title: Advanced PD Controller Parameters
pd_advanced.description: Configure advanced PD controller parameters for expert tuning of battery charge/discharge behavior. Only modify these if you understand PID control theory. Default values work well for most installations.

pd_advanced_config.title: PD Controller Configuration
pd_advanced_config.description: Tune the Proportional-Derivative controller that manages battery power adjustments.

data_description.pd_kp: Responsiveness to grid imbalance. Higher values = faster response but risk of overshoot. Range: 0.1-2.0, default: 0.65
data_description.pd_kd: Damping to prevent oscillation. Higher values = smoother transitions but slower settling. Range: 0.0-2.0, default: 0.5
data_description.pd_deadband: Grid power tolerance around zero. Prevents micro-adjustments to minor fluctuations. Range: 0-200W, default: 40W
data_description.pd_max_power_change: Maximum battery power change per control cycle (2.5s). Prevents abrupt commands. Range: 100-2000W, default: 800W
data_description.pd_direction_hysteresis: Power threshold required to switch between charging and discharging. Prevents rapid direction changes. Range: 0-200W, default: 60W
data_description.pd_min_charge_power: Minimum power for charging. Below this threshold, the controller stays idle instead of charging at low power. 0 = disabled.
data_description.pd_min_discharge_power: Minimum power for discharging. Below this threshold, the controller stays idle instead of discharging at low power. 0 = disabled.
data_description.pd_target_grid_power: Grid power setpoint the controller regulates to. Negative = export to grid, positive = import from grid, 0 = net zero. Range: -500 to +500 W, default: 0 W.
data_description.enable_system_power_limits: When enabled, the two sliders below cap the combined charge/discharge power of all active batteries.
data_description.system_max_charge_power: Optional cap for combined charge power across all active batteries. 0 = disabled; per-battery limits still apply.
data_description.system_max_discharge_power: Optional cap for combined discharge power across all active batteries. 0 = disabled; per-battery limits still apply.

hourly_balance_config.title: Hourly Balance Configuration
hourly_balance_config.description: Configure the target net energy balance and control parameters. The system will adjust battery power to achieve the target Wh balance each hour.

data_description.hourly_balance_target_net_wh: Target net energy balance per hour. 0 = net zero (default). Positive = allow import, negative = target export.
data_description.hourly_balance_max_offset_w: Maximum power offset the controller can apply. The slider ceiling is the combined discharge power of all configured batteries.
data_description.hourly_balance_deadband_wh: Tolerance band around the target. No correction is applied when the net balance is within ±N kWh of the target. 0 = exact correction.
data_description.hourly_balance_hysteresis_w: Minimum change in offset required before a new correction is applied. Prevents micro-adjustments every cycle. 0 = apply every cycle. Default: 15 W.

reconfigure_battery.title: Reconfigure battery {battery_num} — Connection (Marstek)
reconfigure_battery.description: Update connection details for battery {battery_num}. Power limits, SOC limits and all other settings are preserved.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.host: New IP address of the battery on your local network. Leave empty if you use a serial port.
data_description.slave_id: Modbus slave/unit id (default 1). Change only when a Modbus proxy maps several batteries to one IP:port using distinct slave ids.
data_description.serial_port: USB-RS485 adapter path, e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 or COM3. Fill this instead of the IP address to use Modbus RTU over serial; leave empty for TCP.
data_description.queued_gateway_compatibility: For Venus v2 over Modbus TCP only. Restores MVEM's retry path: one send per transaction ID and up to three attempts with a fresh ID after a failed, timed-out or incomplete response. Leave disabled for direct connections, Elfin EW11, serial RTU and normal gateways.

reconfigure_battery_zendure.title: Reconfigure battery {battery_num} — Connection (Zendure)
reconfigure_battery_zendure.description: Update connection details for the Zendure SolarFlow battery {battery_num}. All other settings are preserved.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.host: New IP address of the Zendure SolarFlow on your local network

reconfigure_battery_esphome.title: Reconfigure battery {battery_num} — Connection (LilyGo/ESPHome)
reconfigure_battery_esphome.description: Update the ESPHome device for Marstek battery {battery_num}. All other settings are preserved.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.esphome_device: The LilyGo T-CAN485 device flashed with the Marstek ESPHome firmware

restore_backup.title: Restore previous configuration
restore_backup.description: Found a saved backup of {count} previous configuration(s) (connection, options and tuning). Restore it to recover everything without setting it up again? Your entities and history reconnect automatically.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the battery. Check the IP address and that the battery is accessible on the network.
error.esphome_entities_missing: The selected ESPHome device is missing required entities: {missing}. Make sure it runs the marstek-lilygo-rs485 firmware with the stock entity names.
error.sensor_not_found: Solar forecast sensor does not exist. Verify the sensor is available.
error.invalid_unit: Solar forecast sensor must have 'kWh' or 'Wh' as unit.
error.incomplete_window: Each extra charging window needs both a start and an end time. Fill both, or leave both empty to skip it.
error.overlapping_slots: This time slot overlaps with an existing one for the same battery. Adjust the times, days, or battery scope.
error.midnight_crossing: Start time must be before end time. For overnight periods, create two separate slots (e.g., 22:00-23:59 and 00:00-06:00).
error.slot_does_nothing: The slot must allow at least one direction (charge or discharge).
error.manual_requires_power: Manual mode requires the power override tick to be enabled.
error.no_price_data: Selected sensor does not have the expected price attributes. Nordpool: needs `raw_today` attribute. PVPC: needs `price_00h` … `price_23h` attributes. CKW: needs `prices` attribute with a list of slots. EPEX: needs `data` attribute with a list of slots. ENTSO-e: needs `prices_today` attribute with a list of slots.
error.tibber_unavailable: The Tibber integration is not installed, or its `tibber.get_prices` service is unavailable. Install and configure Tibber first.
error.host_or_serial_required: Enter either an IP address (TCP) or a serial port path (Modbus RTU).

abort.no_legacy_entries: No legacy Marstek Venus configuration was found to migrate.
abort.migration_successful: Migrated {count} configuration(s) to Omnibattery. Your entities, history and settings were preserved.
abort.restore_successful: Restored {count} configuration(s). Your entities, history and settings were recovered.

init.title: Reconfigure Omnibattery
init.description: Select which section you want to configure.

sensors.title: Sensors
sensors.description: Configure the sensors used by the integration.

data_description.consumption_sensor: Sensor that indicates imported (+) or exported (-) power to the grid.
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). If configured here, it will be used automatically for predictive charging and solar charge delay.
data_description.solar_production_sensor: Real-time PV production power sensor (W/kW) from an external inverter not wired through the battery MPPT inputs. Used to show the Solar node in the dashboard energy-flow diagram. Leave empty if your panels feed the battery MPPT directly.
data_description.meter_inverted: Enable this option if your meter uses the opposite convention: positive = export, negative = import. The unit (W/kW) is detected automatically.
data_description.max_contracted_power: Total contracted power in watts. System won't exceed this limit when charging to avoid tripping the breaker

batteries.title: Number of batteries
batteries.description: Modify the number of batteries. If you reduce the number, the last configured batteries will be removed.

battery_brand.title: Battery {battery_num} — Brand
battery_brand.description: Select the brand and type of battery {battery_num}.

data_description.brand: Marstek Venus: Modbus TCP. Zendure SolarFlow: local HTTP (enable HEMS in the Zendure app first).

battery_connection.title: Configure battery {battery_num} — Connection (Marstek)
battery_connection.description: Modify connection details and battery model for battery {battery_num}.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.host: IP address of the battery on your local network. Leave empty if you use a serial port.
data_description.slave_id: Modbus slave/unit id (default 1). Change only when a Modbus proxy maps several batteries to one IP:port using distinct slave ids.
data_description.battery_version: Select your battery model version. Ev2 for older models, Ev3 for newer models with updated register map, A (Venus A) or D (Venus D) for hybrid inverter models
data_description.serial_port: USB-RS485 adapter path, e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 or COM3. Fill this instead of the IP address to use Modbus RTU over serial; leave empty for TCP.
data_description.queued_gateway_compatibility: For Venus v2 over Modbus TCP only. Restores MVEM's retry path: one send per transaction ID and up to three attempts with a fresh ID after a failed, timed-out or incomplete response. Leave disabled for direct connections, Elfin EW11, serial RTU and normal gateways.

battery_connection_zendure.title: Configure battery {battery_num} — Connection (Zendure)
battery_connection_zendure.description: Modify connection details for the Zendure SolarFlow battery {battery_num}. Make sure HEMS is enabled in the Zendure app.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.host: IP address of the Zendure device on your local network

battery_connection_esphome.title: Configure battery {battery_num} — Connection (LilyGo/ESPHome)
battery_connection_esphome.description: Select the ESPHome device (LilyGo RS485 bridge) that exposes Marstek battery {battery_num}. It must run the marstek-lilygo-rs485 firmware with the stock entity names.

data_description.name: Descriptive name to identify this battery
data_description.esphome_device: The LilyGo T-CAN485 device flashed with the Marstek ESPHome firmware

battery_limits.title: Configure battery {battery_num} — Limits
battery_limits.description: Modify power and SOC limits for battery {battery_num}.

data_description.max_charge_power: Maximum power allowed to charge the battery
data_description.max_discharge_power: Maximum power allowed to discharge the battery
data_description.charge_hysteresis_percent: Always on (minimum 2%). After reaching the top, the battery won't charge again until SOC drops to (Max SOC − hysteresis). Ex: Max SOC 100% with 2% = no charge until 98%.
data_description.backup_offgrid_threshold: Offgrid load above this value is treated as an active backup event, excluding the battery from PD control. Set higher if you have permanent loads (router, cameras) on the offgrid port.
data_description.full_charge_voltage_taper_enabled: When the target is 100%, limit charge to 95 W from 3.48 V max cell voltage and stop at 3.58 V to measure imbalance after 60 seconds.
data_description.battery_capacity_kwh: Nominal usable capacity of the battery pack. Used to calculate stored energy and efficiency from SOC. Leave at 0 if unknown.

time_slots.title: Discharge time slots
time_slots.description: Do you want to reconfigure time slots? If unchecked, all slots will be removed (batteries will operate at all times).

add_time_slot.title: Time slot {slot_num}
add_time_slot.description: Define when and how the batteries are allowed to operate. The ticks control each direction, SOC and power. Manual mode forces an exact power, bypassing the PD algorithm.

data_description.start_time: Slot start time (24h). Midnight crossing supported.
data_description.battery_scope: Select 'all' to apply to every battery, or pick a specific one.
data_description.allow_charge: If checked, charge is allowed during the slot. If NO slot allows charge, charge is unrestricted by default.
data_description.allow_discharge: If checked, discharge is allowed during the slot. If NO slot allows discharge, discharge is unrestricted by default.
data_description.soc_override_enabled: If checked, the slot overrides the battery's max/min SOC inside the window.
data_description.power_override_enabled: If checked, the slot limits or forces the max power.
data_description.mode: PD: the predictive algorithm caps power using the slot value. Manual: forced discharge or charge at the configured power.

add_time_slot_details.title: Slot {slot_num} details
add_time_slot_details.description: Configure SOC and/or power limits per battery. Every battery has its own sliders. Mapping: {battery_map}. SOC min range: 12–30%. SOC max range: 80–100%. Power range: 100 W up to each battery’s hardware max.

add_more_slots.title: Add more time slots
add_more_slots.description: You have {current_slots} configured slot(s). You can add up to {max_slots} in total.

excluded_devices.title: Special Device Management
excluded_devices.description: Configure devices with special management: you can EXCLUDE devices that should NOT be powered by battery, or ADD devices that SHOULD be powered by battery even if they're not in the home consumption sensor.

add_excluded_device.title: Special Device {device_num}
add_excluded_device.description: Configure how the battery should manage this device.

data.included_in_consumption: Consumption is included in home consumption sensor
data.allow_solar_surplus: Allow to use solar surplus (do not charge battery)

data_description.power_sensor: Power sensor (W) for the device. For EV chargers without power telemetry, select the state sensor that reads 'Charging' when the vehicle is charging.
data_description.included_in_consumption: ✓ CHECKED = Home sensor ALREADY includes this device → Battery will NOT power it (excluded). ✗ UNCHECKED = Home sensor doesn't see it → Battery WILL power it (additional)
data_description.allow_solar_surplus: If checked, the device will be able to consume energy directly from solar panels (surplus) without the battery trying to compensate. Recommended for high consumption devices like EV chargers.
data_description.ev_charger_no_telemetry: ✓ CHECKED = The selected sensor reports the charging state ('Charging'/'Cargando'). When EV charging is detected, the battery pauses for 5 minutes so the vehicle can draw full power; afterwards the battery may only charge from solar surplus and will never discharge while the EV is charging.

add_more_excluded_devices.title: Add more excluded devices
add_more_excluded_devices.description: You have {current_devices} excluded device(s). You can add up to {max_devices} in total.

predictive_charging.title: Predictive Grid Charging
predictive_charging.description: Do you want to enable intelligent grid charging? This feature allows charging batteries from the grid during off-peak hours when today's solar forecast is insufficient.

predictive_charging_mode.title: Predictive Charging Mode
predictive_charging_mode.description: Select how the system decides when to charge from the grid.

data_description.predictive_charging_mode: Time Slot: charge during a fixed nightly window. Dynamic Pricing: automatically select the cheapest hours from a price sensor. Real-Time Price: activate charging instantly whenever the current price is below a threshold — no scheduling needed.

predictive_charging_config.title: Predictive Charging — Time Slot
predictive_charging_config.description: Configure when and how to charge batteries from the grid based on solar forecast. You can set up to 3 charging windows — fill only window 1 to keep a single window, or add windows 2 and 3 as needed.

data_description.start_time: Start time of charging window (e.g., 01:00, off-peak tariff)
data_description.days: Days when grid charging is allowed
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing the solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). If you have solar panels, configure this sensor — the system will only charge from the grid when solar forecast is insufficient. If you have no solar panels, leave this empty — the system will charge whenever battery energy is not enough for the day. Not required if you already configured this sensor in the initial setup step — it will be used automatically.
data_description.predictive_safety_margin_kwh: Extra energy buffer added to the consumption forecast before deciding whether to charge. Useful when your solar forecast tends to be optimistic. Set to 0 to disable (default). Capped at total battery capacity.
data_description.predictive_grid_charge_margin_pct: Extra % charged from the grid on top of the solar-deficit, to hedge against optimistic solar forecasts. E.g. a 2 kWh grid need at 50% charges 3 kWh. 0 to disable (default). Capped at the gap to max SOC.

dynamic_pricing_config.title: Predictive Charging — Dynamic Pricing
dynamic_pricing_config.description: Configure the electricity price sensor and parameters for automatic cheap-hour selection.

data.dp_price_discharge_control: Only discharge when price is above threshold

data_description.price_integration_type: Select your electricity price provider integration. Tibber is service-based: it reads prices from the tibber.get_prices service, so the price sensor below can be left empty.
data_description.price_sensor: Sensor providing electricity prices (e.g., sensor.nordpool_kwh_es_eur_3_10_0 for Nordpool, sensor.esios_pvpc for PVPC, or your CKW sensor for Switzerland)
data_description.max_price_threshold: Slots above this price will not be selected. When price-gated discharge is enabled, this value is also used as the discharge threshold. Use the same unit as the sensor: €/kWh for Nordpool/PVPC, CHF/kWh for CKW. Leave empty to use all slots and use the daily average as the discharge threshold.
data_description.dp_price_discharge_control: When enabled, the battery only discharges when the current electricity price exceeds the configured maximum price threshold. If no threshold is configured, it uses today's average price computed automatically from the price sensor. If discharge time slots are configured, both conditions must be met.
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing the solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). If you have solar panels, configure this sensor — the system will only charge from the grid when solar forecast is insufficient. If you have no solar panels, leave this empty — the system will charge whenever battery energy is not enough for the day. Not required if you already configured this sensor in the initial setup step — it will be used automatically.
data_description.predictive_safety_margin_kwh: Extra energy buffer added to the consumption forecast before deciding whether to charge. Useful when your solar forecast tends to be optimistic. Set to 0 to disable (default). Capped at total battery capacity.
data_description.predictive_grid_charge_margin_pct: Extra % charged from the grid on top of the solar-deficit, to hedge against optimistic solar forecasts. E.g. a 2 kWh grid need at 50% charges 3 kWh. 0 to disable (default). Capped at the gap to max SOC.

realtime_price_config.title: Predictive Charging — Real-Time Price
realtime_price_config.description: Configure the price sensor and threshold. The system reads the current price every few seconds and starts or stops grid charging immediately — no daily schedule needed.

data.rt_price_discharge_control: Only discharge when price is above threshold

data_description.price_sensor: Sensor whose state is the current electricity price (e.g. sensor.esios_pvpc, sensor.nordpool_kwh_es_eur_3_10_0). Any sensor that exposes the current period price as its state works.
data_description.max_price_threshold: Charging activates when the current price is at or below this value. Use the same unit as your price sensor (e.g. €/kWh for Nordpool/PVPC, CHF/kWh for CKW). Leave empty if using the daily average sensor below.
data_description.average_price_sensor: If provided, this sensor's state is used as the threshold instead of the fixed value above — useful when your price integration already exposes a daily average. Leave empty to use the fixed threshold.
data_description.rt_price_discharge_control: When enabled, the battery only discharges when the current electricity price exceeds the threshold (fixed or daily average). If discharge time slots are configured, both conditions must be met.
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing the solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). If you have solar panels, configure this sensor — the system will only charge from the grid when solar forecast is insufficient. Not required if you already configured this sensor in the initial setup step — it will be used automatically.
data_description.predictive_safety_margin_kwh: Extra energy buffer added to the consumption forecast before deciding whether to charge. Useful when your solar forecast tends to be optimistic. Set to 0 to disable (default). Capped at total battery capacity.
data_description.predictive_grid_charge_margin_pct: Extra % charged from the grid on top of the solar-deficit, to hedge against optimistic solar forecasts. E.g. a 2 kWh grid need at 50% charges 3 kWh. 0 to disable (default). Capped at the gap to max SOC.

weekly_full_charge.title: Weekly Full Charge
weekly_full_charge.description: Do you want to enable weekly full battery charge for cell balancing? This feature charges batteries to 100% once per week to balance the cells.

weekly_full_charge_config.title: Weekly Full Charge Configuration
weekly_full_charge_config.description: Select the day of the week when batteries should charge to 100% for cell balancing. After reaching 100%, the system reverts to your configured maximum charge limit.

data_description.weekly_full_charge_day: Day when batteries will charge to 100% regardless of configured max SOC. This helps balance battery cells.

charge_delay.title: Solar charge delay
charge_delay.description: Enable solar charge delay? This delays battery charging until the solar energy balance indicates it's needed, exporting excess solar to grid in the meantime.

charge_delay_config.title: Charge delay configuration
charge_delay_config.description: Configure the safety margin and solar forecast sensor for the charge delay.

data_description.delay_safety_margin_h: Hours before sunset by which charging must be complete. Higher values unlock charging earlier.
data_description.delay_soc_setpoint_enabled: When enabled, the battery charges freely up to the configured SOC; above it the solar delay activates.
data_description.delay_soc_setpoint: The SOC the battery must reach before the solar delay kicks in. Minimum is 12 % — the Venus battery minimum discharge SOC.
data_description.solar_forecast_sensor: Sensor providing solar production forecast in kWh (e.g., Solcast). Not required if you already configured this sensor in the initial setup step — it will be used automatically.

temp_charge_limit.title: Temperature charge limit
temp_charge_limit.description: Reduce charge power when a battery gets hot? Above a temperature limit charging is throttled proportionally and restored as the battery cools.

temp_charge_limit_config.title: Temperature charge limit configuration
temp_charge_limit_config.description: Set the temperature at which charging starts to throttle, the band over which it ramps down, and the minimum charge power (as a percentage of normal).

data_description.temp_charge_limit_c: Charging runs at full power at or below this temperature; above it the derate begins.
data_description.temp_charge_limit_band_c: Temperature range above the limit over which charge power ramps down to the minimum.
data_description.temp_charge_limit_floor_pct: Charge power at the limit plus the band, as a percentage of the normal charge ceiling. 0% stops charging entirely when very hot.
data_description.temp_limit_apply_discharge: Apply the same temperature derate to discharge power. Discharge tolerates heat better, so this shares the charge threshold as a compromise; mainly it keeps discharge under the BMS hard cutoff.

capacity_protection.title: Capacity Protection Mode
capacity_protection.description: When enabled, if battery SOC drops below a threshold, the system conserves energy by only discharging to offset consumption above a peak limit.

capacity_protection_config.title: Capacity Protection Configuration
capacity_protection_config.description: Configure the SOC threshold below which protection activates, and the peak power limit. The battery will only discharge to keep grid import at or below the peak limit.

data_description.capacity_protection_soc_threshold: When average battery SOC drops below this value, capacity protection activates. The battery will stop discharging for normal consumption and only cover peaks above the limit.
data_description.capacity_protection_limit: Grid import power threshold. When house consumption exceeds this value and protection is active, the battery discharges only the excess above this limit.

pd_advanced.title: Advanced PD Controller Parameters
pd_advanced.description: Configure advanced PD controller parameters for expert tuning of battery charge/discharge behavior. Only modify these if you understand PID control theory. Default values work well for most installations.

pd_advanced_config.title: PD Controller Configuration
pd_advanced_config.description: Tune the Proportional-Derivative controller that manages battery power adjustments. Changes will apply after integration reload.

data_description.pd_kp: Responsiveness to grid imbalance. Higher values = faster response but risk of overshoot. Range: 0.1-2.0, default: 0.65
data_description.pd_kd: Damping to prevent oscillation. Higher values = smoother transitions but slower settling. Range: 0.0-2.0, default: 0.5
data_description.pd_deadband: Grid power tolerance around zero. Prevents micro-adjustments to minor fluctuations. Higher values reduce sensitivity. Range: 0-200W, default: 40W
data_description.pd_max_power_change: Maximum battery power change per control cycle (2.5s). Prevents abrupt commands. Lower values = smoother but slower. Range: 100-2000W, default: 800W
data_description.pd_direction_hysteresis: Power threshold required to switch between charging and discharging. Prevents rapid direction changes. Range: 0-200W, default: 60W
data_description.pd_min_charge_power: Minimum power for charging. Below this threshold, the controller stays idle instead of charging at low power. 0 = disabled.
data_description.pd_min_discharge_power: Minimum power for discharging. Below this threshold, the controller stays idle instead of discharging at low power. 0 = disabled.
data_description.pd_target_grid_power: Grid power setpoint the controller regulates to. Negative = export to grid, positive = import from grid, 0 = net zero. Range: -500 to +500 W, default: 0 W.
data_description.enable_system_power_limits: When enabled, the two sliders below cap the combined charge/discharge power of all active batteries.
data_description.system_max_charge_power: Optional cap for combined charge power across all active batteries. 0 = disabled; per-battery limits still apply.
data_description.system_max_discharge_power: Optional cap for combined discharge power across all active batteries. 0 = disabled; per-battery limits still apply.

hourly_balance.title: Hourly Net Balance
hourly_balance.description:
Enable hourly net balance control? This feature tracks grid import/export per hour and automatically adjusts the battery setpoint to achieve a target net energy balance.

⚠️ **This feature is only useful in Spain**, under the hourly surplus-compensation scheme (Royal Decree 244/2019), where grid surplus is settled hour by hour. In markets with feed-in tariffs or annual net metering it provides no benefit and may cause lost export revenue and unnecessary battery cycling.

Detected Home Assistant country: **{country}**.

hourly_balance_config.title: Hourly Balance Configuration
hourly_balance_config.description: Configure the target net energy balance and control parameters. The system will adjust battery power to achieve the target Wh balance each hour.

data_description.hourly_balance_target_net_wh: Target net energy balance per hour. 0 = net zero (default). Positive = allow import, negative = target export.
data_description.hourly_balance_max_offset_w: Maximum power offset the controller can apply. The slider ceiling is the combined discharge power of all configured batteries.
data_description.hourly_balance_deadband_wh: Tolerance band around the target. No correction is applied when the net balance is within ±N kWh of the target. 0 = exact correction.
data_description.hourly_balance_hysteresis_w: Minimum change in offset required before a new correction is applied. Prevents micro-adjustments every cycle. 0 = apply every cycle. Default: 15 W.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the battery. Check the IP address and that the battery is accessible on the network.
error.esphome_entities_missing: The selected ESPHome device is missing required entities: {missing}. Make sure it runs the marstek-lilygo-rs485 firmware with the stock entity names.
error.sensor_not_found: Solar forecast sensor does not exist. Verify the sensor is available.
error.invalid_unit: Solar forecast sensor must have 'kWh' or 'Wh' as unit.
error.incomplete_window: Each extra charging window needs both a start and an end time. Fill both, or leave both empty to skip it.
error.overlapping_slots: This time slot overlaps with an existing one for the same battery. Adjust the times, days, or battery scope.
error.midnight_crossing: Start time must be before end time. For overnight periods, create two separate slots (e.g., 22:00-23:59 and 00:00-06:00).
error.slot_does_nothing: The slot must allow at least one direction (charge or discharge).
error.manual_requires_power: Manual mode requires the power override tick to be enabled.
error.no_price_data: Selected sensor does not have the expected price attributes. Nordpool: needs `raw_today` attribute. PVPC: needs `price_00h` … `price_23h` attributes. CKW: needs `prices` attribute with a list of slots. EPEX: needs `data` attribute with a list of slots. ENTSO-e: needs `prices_today` attribute with a list of slots.
error.tibber_unavailable: The Tibber integration is not installed, or its `tibber.get_prices` service is unavailable. Install and configure Tibber first.
error.host_or_serial_required: Enter either an IP address (TCP) or a serial port path (Modbus RTU).
